title: legis governance_read.v1
description: PROVENANCE: legis OWNS this contract (legis is the governance authority); warpline mirrors it VERBATIM as the source of truth for its advisory LegisGovernanceClient consumer and NEVER gates on it. v1 reports VERIFIED GOVERNANCE CLEARANCES ONLY, keyed on SEI: an operator-override verdict (OVERRIDDEN_BY_OPERATOR) or a sign-off an operator cleared, each signed. It deliberately does NOT report in-flight/uncleared governance: open (PENDING) sign-off requests, BLOCKED verdicts awaiting an operator, or Filigree bindings. CONSEQUENCE warpline must disclose, not hide: records:[] under status:checked means 'legis holds no verified CLEARANCE for this SEI' — which conflates three distinct states legis cannot tell apart (truly ungoverned; a known SEI with a clearance never recorded; AND an entity actively BLOCKED awaiting sign-off). So an actively-blocked entity is byte-identical to an ungoverned one in this channel. warpline therefore renders [] as enrichment.governance=absent meaning 'no verified clearance', NEVER 'ungoverned'. Only Loomweave can say a SEI does not exist; legis is an SEI consumer and cannot. Contract is additive within v1; any incompatible change bumps to .v2.

status.description: Envelope-level read status. 'checked' = legis answered (records may be empty); 'unavailable' = legis could not produce a signature-verifiable answer (tampered/unverifiable trail). NOT a verdict, NOT warpline's enrichment scalar.

records.description: The verified clearance records for this SEI. Empty = no verified clearance (see PROVENANCE: this is NOT 'ungoverned' and may hide an actively-blocked entity).

unavailable.description: Present (and non-empty) only when status='unavailable': the machine reason(s) legis could not answer.

disposition.description: Record-level: a v1 record is ALWAYS a clearance. (NOT the envelope status, NOT warpline's enrichment.governance scalar.)

posture.description: The PROVABLE clearance mechanism: a protected-cell operator override, or an operator-cleared sign-off.

as_of.description: Signed recorded_at (RFC3339 UTC; both +00:00 and Z accepted).

reasons.description: Closed-vocab kind codes for the clearance.

content_hash.description: The signed Loomweave content hash binding the clearance to the governed content. warpline ECHOES this verbatim; it is NOT re-derived against the current body (governance is an advisory echo, not a warpline-asserted freshness verdict — contrast the attest-2 risk-as-verification path).
